steps.title: Identify the restrictive name
steps.content: In "fellow OECD member nation Finland," the country name specifies which member nation is meant. Because the name is essential, no punctuation should separate it from "nation."

steps.title: Reject set-off punctuation
steps.content: Choice A uses a dash, and Choice C uses a comma; both wrongly set off the essential country name. Choice B uses a colon even though the words before the blank are not an independent clause. The remaining option leaves the restrictive name unpunctuated. The answer is D.
